scenario_description: A user documents the process of packing away a specific black-and-white family portrait. The photo moves from a desk to a box, then is placed on a shelf next to a similar-looking but distinct color photo.

turns.text: Here's what I'm clearing off the desk first.
turns.image_description: A cluttered wooden desk surface. Leaning against a messy stack of yellowed old newspapers is a framed black-and-white photograph. The frame is dark wood with a simple bevel. Inside the glass, the photo shows three people standing in front of a vintage car; it is strictly monochrome. To the right of the newspaper stack sits a red ceramic mug.

turns.text: That looks like a nice family memory. Is that the only thing you're taking down?
Yeah, just putting everything into this box for now.
turns.image_description: An open cardboard moving box sitting on the same wooden desk. The stack of newspapers and the red mug are gone. Inside the box, lying face-down, is the same dark-wood framed black-and-white photograph from the previous turn. It is surrounded by four other unframed photographs of similar size, all also lying face-down. The back of the frames/photos are visible, showing standard white matting or backing paper.

turns.text: Got it packed away safely. Are you going to keep them all in there or sort through them later?
Actually, I took one out and put it on the shelf here. Which one is the original picture from the desk?
turns.image_description: A wooden bookshelf. On the middle shelf, two framed photographs stand side-by-side. On the left is a framed color photograph of a modern beach scene with blue water and sand. On the right is a framed black-and-white photograph showing three people in front of a vintage car (identical to the content of the photo in Turn 1). Both frames are dark wood. The lighting is soft indoor light.

turns.text: I see both frames on the shelf. They look similar in style, but the contents are quite different.
Which one is the original picture from the desk? A) The color beach scene on the left B) The black-and-white photo on the right C) Neither, they are both new photos D) The one behind the books

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 establishes the 'original picture' as a framed black-and-white photo of three people in front of a vintage car. Turn 2 confirms this specific item was moved into a box. Turn 5 shows a shelf with two options: a color beach photo and a black-and-white photo matching the description from Turn 1. To answer correctly, the model must track the visual identity of the object from Turn 1 across the intermediate turns to identify it among the distractors in Turn 5.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The difficulty lies in maintaining the visual identity of the specific entity (the B&W car photo) across three distinct scenes where it changes context (leaning on papers -> face down in a box -> standing on a shelf) and distinguishing it from a visually similar distractor (another framed photo) in the final image without relying on explicit text labels.

_meta.scenario: Turn 1 displays a framed black-and-white photograph leaning against a stack of old newspapers on a desk. Turn 2 shows the desk cleared, but the photograph is now lying face-down inside an open cardboard box filled with other similarly sized family photos.
